text: Circu lar No- t 080/0 I 12022-CX. F. No- 296163n020-CX.9 Government of India Department of Revenue Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs ********* Room No-267D, North Block, New Delhi, lgth January,2022 All Principal chief commissioners/chief commissioners of sr & cx, AII Principal Chief Commissioners/chief commissioners of Customs All Principal Director Generals/Director Generals, CBIC Sub: Writing-off of arrears of Central Excise Duty, Service Tax and Customs Duty -Constitution of Committees to advise the authority for writing off of arrears - reg. Madam/Sir With reference to writing off of arears of Central Excise duty, Service Tax and Customs duty, the following instructions are issued in supersession of guidelines communicated vide Circular No. 1059/812017'Cx dated 26.09.2017, Corrigendum dated 20.10.2017 and Instructions dated 07.03.2018' in exercise of powers under Delegation of Financial power Rules. l97g as amended vide SO 3624 dated 14.12.2012. 2' The Committee of Pr. Chief Commissioners/ Chief Commissioners and principal Commissioners/ Commissioners constituted in the respective Zones, shall exercise the powers as specified in Column-2 below:- I o) Authority Monetary limit up to which the loss may be written offin each case Chief Commissioner/ Chief Commissioner of Customs and/ or CGST and CX as specitied in Annexure A (for dues of CX and ST) and Annexure B (for dues of Customs) to this Circular (i) Pr amounts of fines and penalties imposed under the Customs Act, 1962, the Central- Excise Act, 1944, the Gold Control Act, 1968. and the Finance Act,1994 and (b) To write-off irrecoverable amounts of Customs (a) Full powers for abandonment of irrecoverable subject or Central Excise or Tax Service to duty Rs. up 5 I lakhs to a to next the report higher of Pr. Commil sioner/ Commissioner of Customs and/ or CGST and CX as constituted by the respective Pr. Chief Commissionerl Chief Commissioner of the Zone. (ii) Committee amounts of fines and penalties imposed under the Customs Act, 1962, the Central Excise Act, 1944, the Gold Control Act, 1968, and the Finance Act, 1994; and (b) To write-off irrecoverable amounts of Customs or Central Excise Duty or Service Tax up to Rs. l0 lakhs subject to a report to the next higher authority.
